info.description:
Welcome to the Gr4vy API reference documentation.
Our API is still very much a work in product and subject to change.
info.title: Gr4vy API (Beta)

post.description:
Registers a card with all available processors.

This API returns asynchronously, returning a pending `Status` object with the
ID of the pending card resource.

Attempts to create an authorization for a payment method.

This endpoint accepts a few additional fields that allow for simultanious
capturing and storage of the payment method.

* `payment_method.store` - Use this field to store the payment method for
future use. Not all payment methods support this feature.
* `capture` - Use this method to also perform a capture of the transaction
after it has been authorized.

The body of this API takes a list of items as a JSON object.

This API returns asynchronously, returning a list of status objects with the
IDs of the pending `Transaction` resources.

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.
parameters.name: transaction_id

post.description:
Authorize an existing but pending transaction.

This endpoint is used when a transaction was previously created that required
additional information, or a redirect, from the buyer. Use this endpoint to
post the additional information to trigger the transaction to be properly
authorized.

Additionally, this endpoint will capture the transaction and store the payment
method if this was previously specified when creating the transaction. We
currently do not support overwritting these settings at this point.

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.
parameters.name: transaction_id

post.description:
Captures a previously authorized transaction.

This API returns asynchronously, returning status object with the ID of the
pending `Transaction` resource.

get.description: Returns a list of rulesets that can be used for card transactions.

400.description: Returns an error if the ruleset is not valid JSON

post.description: Creates a new ruleset that can be used for card transactions.

parameters.description: The unique ID for a card ruleset.
parameters.name: card_ruleset_id

403.description: Returns an error if the ruleset could not be deleted

404.description: Returns an error if the ruleset could not be found
Returns an error if the ruleset could not be found

get.description: Returns a rulesets that can be used for card transactions.

400.description: Returns an error if the ruleset is not valid JSON

404.description: Returns an error if the ruleset could not be found

put.description: Updates a ruleset that can be used for card transactions.

parameters.description: The unique ID for a card ruleset.
parameters.name: card_ruleset_id

403.description: Returns an error if the ruleset is not valid

404.description: Returns an error if the ruleset could not be found

post.description:
Activates aa ruleset for card transactions, making it the one
ruleset used by all card transactions.

Only one ruleset can be active at any time. By activating a ruleset the
existing active ruleset will be deactivated.

Only valid rulesets can be activated.

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description:
The marker that identifies the page of results to return.

For the first page of results, simply omit this parameter.
For next pages, simply use the value returned by the API in
the `next_page` field.
parameters.name: marker

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

201.description: This API returns asynchronously, returning status object with the ID of the pending `Card` resource.

400.description: Returns an error if the request is missing any required fields, or the request body is not valid JSON.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

parameters.description: The unique ID for a card.

Returns a list of all available payment service definitions.

get.description: Gets the definition for a payment service.

put.description: Makes an update to a payment service definition

get.description: Lists the currently configured and activated payment services for a for a merchant.

post.description: Adds a new payment service by providing a custom name and a value for each of the required fields.

parameters.description: The ID of the payment service
parameters.name: payment_service_id

delete.description: Deletes a specific active payment service.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a single configured payment service.

put.description: Updates an existing payment service. Allows all fields to be changed except for the service ID.

parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description:
The marker that identifies the offsett for the page of results to return.

For the first page of results, simply omit this parameter. For next pages,
simply use the value returned by the API in the `next_page` field.

A marker is an opague string that is only meaningful to ur API. No
additional value can be ascribed to this value.
parameters.name: marker

parameters.description: Filters the results to just the transactions that match this external identifier or transaction ID.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Filters the results to only transactions last updated before this ISO date-time string.
parameters.name: before_updated_at

parameters.description: Filters the results to only transactions last updated after this ISO date-time string.
parameters.name: after_updated_at

parameters.description: Filters the results to only transactions created before this ISO date-time string.
parameters.name: before_created_at

parameters.description: Filters the results to only transactions created after this ISO date-time string.
parameters.name: after_created_at

parameters.description: Filters the results to only transactions with as specific transaction status.
parameters.name: transaction_status

200.description: Returns a paginated list of transactions for an account.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

get.description: Lists all transactions for an account. Sorted by last `updated_at` status.

400.description: Returns an error if the request is missing any required fields, or the request body is not valid JSON.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

post.description:
Attempts to create an authorization for a payment method. In some cases it is
not possible to create the authorization without redirectingt the user for
their authorization. In these cases the status is set to
`user_authorization_required` and an `authorization_url` is returned.

Additionally, this endpoint accepts a few additional fields that allow for
simultanious capturing and storage of the payment method.

* `payment_method.store` - Use this field to store the payment method for
furture use. Not all payment methods support this feature.
* `capture` - Use this method to also perform a capture of the transaction
after it has been authorized.

201.description: Returns a list of status objects, one for each requested capture.

400.description: Returns an error if the request is missing any required fields, or the request body is not valid JSON.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

post.description: Takes a list of authorization IDs and processes them as needed.

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.
parameters.name: transaction_id

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

404.description: Returns an error if the transaction can not be found or has not yet been created.

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.
parameters.name: transaction_id

Authorize an existing but pending transaction.

This endpoint is used when a transaction was previously created that required

authorized.

Additionally, this endpoint will capture the transaction and store the payment
method if this was previously specified when creating the transaction. We
currently do not support overwritting these settings at this point.

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.
parameters.name: transaction_id

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.

get.description: Returns a list of events for a given transaction.

created_at.description:
The date and time
when this card was created in our system.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the card against their own records.

id.description: The internal ID for this card

status.description: Wether this card was successfully created or not.

type.description: The type of this resource. Is always `card`

CardCreateRequest.description: A request to store a card.

expiration_date.description:
The expiration date of the card, formatted `MM/YY`. If a card has been
previously stored with us this value is optional.

If the `number` of this card represents a tokenized card, then this value
is ignored.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the card against their own records.

number.description:
The 15-16 digit number for this credit card as it can be found on the
front of the card.

If a card has been stored with us previously, this number will represent
the unique tokenized card ID provided via our API.

security_code.description:
The 3 or 4 digit security code often found on the card. This often
referred to as the CVV or CVD.

If the `number` of this card represents a tokenized card, then this value
is ignored.

CardCreateRequest.title: Card (Create)

CardEvent.description: An event that happened for a transaction.

code.description: The type of event, in dot-format syntax.

external_service.description: An optional related resource that triggered this event.

display_name.description: The user's name for the resource

service_id.description: The ID of the service that this resource is based on.

id.description: The unique ID for this event.

type.description: The type of this resource. Must always be transaction.event

CardEvent.title: Card Event

CardEvents.description: A list of events for a card

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previ page of results.

CardEvents.title: Card Events

CardRuleConditional.description:
A conditional rule in a ruleset. It defines an if/else condition and a rule to
move on to next for each of these values.

condition.description: The comparison to make to `value` property.

false_rule_id.description: the ID of the rule to use if the comparison equals false.

id.description: A unique ID for this rule in the ruleset

key.description: The transation field to filter by

true_rule_id.description: the ID of the rule to use if the comparison equals true.

value.description:
The value to compare the `key` to. This value may be casted to
a number for the currency amount.

CardRuleConditional.title: Conditional Card Rule

CardRuleTerminal.description:
A terminal rule in a ruleset. It the payment services to use in case the
ruleset reaches this node.

id.description: A unique ID for this rule in the ruleset

payment_service_ids.description:
A list of IDs for the payment services to use, in order of priority.
The payment services all need to process cards.

CardRuleTerminal.title: Terminal Card Rule

CardRuleset.description:
A card ruleset which holds the rules that are parsed for a card transaction.

Multiple rulesets can exists at any time, but only one can be deployed.

active.description:
Wether this ruleset is currently in use. Only one ruleset can
be active at any time.

created_at.description: The date and time when this ruleset was created

display_name.description: The display name of the ruleset

root_rule_id.description: The ID of the rule that is the starting point of the ruleset.

rules.description: A list of rules in this ruleset

updated_at.description: The date and time when this ruleset was last updated

valid.description:
Wether this ruleset is valid and complete. Only valid rulesets can be
activated.

CardRuleset.title: Card Ruleset

CardRulesetCreateRequest.description:
Creates a new card ruleset which holds the rules that are parsed for a card
transaction.

display_name.description: The display name of the ruleset

root_rule_id.description: The ID of the rule that is the starting point of the ruleset.

rules.description: A list of rules in this ruleset

CardRulesetCreateRequest.title: Card Ruleset (Create)

CardRulesetUpdateRequest.description:
Updates a card ruleset which holds the rules that are parsed for a card
transaction.

display_name.description: The display name of the ruleset

root_rule_id.description: The ID of the rule that is the starting point of the ruleset.

rules.description: A list of rules in this ruleset

CardRulesetUpdateRequest.title: Card Ruleset (Update)

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previ page of results.

code.description: A custom code to further describe the type of error being returned. This code provides further specification within the HTTP `status` code and can be used by a program to define logic based on the error.

message.description: A human readable message that describes the error. The content of this field should not be used to determine any business logic.
message.example: The request body was not valid JSON.

status.description: The HTTP status code of this error

type.description: The typeof this object. This is always `error`.

PaymentMethod.description: A generic object to hold payment method details.

authorization_url.description: The optional URL that needs to be presented to a consumer to allow them to approve the authorization.

id.description: The optional ID of any stored payment method details, for example a stored `card` ID.

PaymentMethod.title: PaymentMethod

accepted_countries.description: A list of countries for which this service is enabled, in ISO two-letter code format.

accepted_currencies.description: A list of currencies for which this service is enabled, in ISO 4217 three-letter code format.

display_name.description: The custom name set for this service

id.description: The ID of this payment service instance

payment_method_id.description: Defines the ID of the payment method that this service handles.

payment_service_definition_id.description: The ID of the payment service used to create this service instance.

status.description:
The current status of this service. This will start off as pending, move
to created, and might eventually move to an error status if and when the
credentials are no longer valid.

use_test_environment.description: Defines if this service should use the test credentials and/or endpoint.

PaymentServiceCreateRequest.description: Request body for activating a payment service

accepted_countries.description: A list of countries that this payment service needs to support in ISO two-letter code format.

accepted_currencies.description: A list of currencies that this payment service needs to support in ISO 4217 three-letter code format.

display_name.description: A custom name for the payment service. This will be shown in the Admin UI.

payment_service_definition_id.description: The ID of the payment service to use

required_fields.description: A list of fields, each containing a key-value pair for each required field defined by the service for this payment service.

items.description: A field containing a key-value pair for a required field defined by the service for this payment service.

key.description: The key of the field to set a value for.

value.description: The value of a field to set.

use_test_environment.description: Defines if this payment service uses the service's test or sandbox environment. Defaults to false.

PaymentServiceCreateRequest.title: Payment Service (Create)

PaymentServiceDefinition.description: An available payment service that can be configured for this cluster.

available.description: Defines if a service is available to be activated in this cluster.

display_name.description: The display name of this service

help_url.description: A URL that provides more details about this payment service

id.description: The ID of the payment service. This is the underlying provider followed by a dash followed by the payment method ID.

payment_method_id.description: The ID of the payment method that this services handles

required_fields.description: A list of fields that need to be submitted when activating the payment service.

items.description: A single field that needs to be submitted for a payment service when it is created.

display_name.description: The name to display for a field in the cluster UI

help_url.description: A URL that provides more details about this field

key.description: The key of a field that needs to be submitted

supported_countries.description: A list of 2-letter ISO country codes that this service supports

supported_currencies.description: A list of 3-letter ISO currency codes that this service supports

PaymentServiceUpdateRequest.description: Request body for updating an active payment service

accepted_countries.description: A list of countries that this payment service needs to support in ISO two-letter code format.

accepted_currencies.description: A list of currencies that this payment service needs to support in ISO 4217 three-letter code format.

PaymentServices.description: A list of active, configured payment services

Status.description: In many cases the Gr4vy API returns asynchronously, kicking off a job to create an authorization or a transaction, and returning a `Status` object with the ID of the pending object. The ID of this resource can be used to query an objects status, or additionally the client can use Pub/Sub to subscribe to the creation of the pending object.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the card against their own records.

resource_id.description: The ID of the object for which this status has been created.

resource_type.description: The type of the object that is pending.

status.description:
The status of this resource being created. This can be one of:

* `pending` - The creation has not yet completed
* `created` - The resource has been created and can now be queried.

type.description: The type of this object. This is always `status`.

Status.title: Status

amount.description: The currency amount captured by this transaction.

created_at.description: The date and time when this transaction was created in our system.

currency.description: The currency code for this transaction.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the authorization against their own records.

id.description: The unique identifier for this transaction

payment_service_id.description: The ID of the payment service that was used for this transaction.

status.description: Wether this transaction was successfully created or not.

transaction_status.description: The status of the transaction being processed. This is different from the `status` field in that it represents the status of the transaction at the payment processor, not the status of the transaction created in Gr4vy.

type.description: The type of this resource. Is always `transaction`

updated_at.description: Defines when the transaction was last updated

TransactionAuthorizeRequest.description: A request to authorize a transaction

amount.description:
The (partial) amount to capture.

When left blank, this will capture the entire amount.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the authorization against their own records.

TransactionAuthorizeRequest.title: Authorize (Create)

TransactionCaptureRequest.description: A request to capture a transaction

amount.description:
The (partial) amount to capture.

When left blank, this will capture the entire amount.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the capture against their own records.

TransactionCaptureRequest.title: Capture a transaction

TransactionCreateRequest.description: A request to create a transaction

amount.description:
The monitary amount to create an authorization for, in the smallest
currency unit for the given currency, for example `1299` cents to create
an autorization for `$12.99`.

For BitCoin, this value will be a decimal point number, for example
`0.0039`.

capture.description: Wether a successful authorization should immediately be captured as well, effectively processing the payment directly.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used by the merchant to match the authorization against their own records.

payment_method.description: The optional payment method details to create an authorization for. This field is required for processing a card.

payment_method_type.description: The type of payment method to create an authorization for.

use_test_environment.description: Defines if this transaction should run in the payment provider's test environment. Defaults to false.

TransactionCreateRequest.title: Transaction (Create)

TransactionEvent.description: An event that happened for a transaction.

code.description: The type of event, in dot-format syntax.

id.description: The unique ID for this event.

resource.description: An optional related resource that triggered this event.

display_name.description: The user's name for the resource

service_id.description: The ID of the service that this resource is based on.

TransactionEvent.title: Transaction Event

TransactionEvents.description: A list of events for a transaction

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previ page of results.

TransactionEvents.title: Transaction Events

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previ page of results.

TransactionsBatchCaptureRequest.description: A request to capture multiple previously authorized transactions.

transaction_id.description: The ID of the transaction to capture

TransactionsBatchCaptureRequest.title: Batch Capture (Create)
